req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: source.owner == target.owner

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a daily transfer limit of $25,000 for internal account transfers. Test design applies: EP to validate a nominal valid transfer well within the limit; BVA to probe the exact $25,000 boundary (inclusive upper bound) and $25,000.01 (first value over limit); NEG for insufficient funds (409) and unauthorized cross-customer transfer (403/422); DT to combine limit-exceeded with otherwise-valid inputs; STC to verify account balance state transitions after a successful transfer. Together these achieve full coverage of the stated positive, negative, and boundary paths mandated by R-FIN-053.

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account has balance >= $1,000
Target account belongs to the same customer
No prior transfers have been made today (daily total = $0)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-053: customer may transfer up to $25,000/day
EP: amount 1000.00 is a valid representative of the partition [0.01, 25000.00]
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "1000.00"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "completed"

    src_bal_after = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_after = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    assert src_bal_after == src_bal_before - 1000.00
    assert tgt_bal_after == tgt_bal_before + 1000.00

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account has balance >= $25,000
Target account belongs to the same customer
No prior transfers have been made today (daily total = $0)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Daily limit counter equals $25,000 after transfer
trace: R-FIN-053: transfers above $25,000/day shall be declined — therefore $25,000 must be accepted
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.00"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected success at boundary, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "completed"

    daily_used = api_client.get_daily_transfer_total(src)
    assert daily_used == 25000.00, f"Daily total mismatch: {daily_used}"

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_is_declined
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account has balance >= $25,000.01
Target account belongs to the same customer
No prior transfers have been made today (daily total = $0)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No funds moved (source and target balances unchanged)
trace: R-FIN-053: transfers above $25,000/day shall be declined with 422
BVA: first OUT-of-range value above upper boundary = 25000.01
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_is_declined(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.01"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account balance is $50.00
Target account belongs to the same customer
No prior transfers have been made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=500.00 (exceeds source balance), source_account_id, target_account_id
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-053 error pathway: 409 on insufficient funds
NEG: invalid input partition — transfer amount exceeds available source balance
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, own_accounts_low_balance, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_low_balance  # src balance == 50.00
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "500.00"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "insufficient_funds"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == 50.00
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_cross_customer_accounts_is_unauthorized
preconditions: Authenticated customer A session token available via <auth_fixture>
source_account belongs to customer A
target_account belongs to customer B (different owner)
Source account has sufficient funds
actions: POST /transfers/internal using customer A token with source=A account, target=B account
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body error code indicates 'unauthorized' or 'account_ownership_mismatch'
trace: R-FIN-053 precondition: source.owner == target.owner must hold
NEG: precondition violation — cross-customer transfer attempt
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_cross_customer_accounts_is_unauthorized(api_client, cross_customer_accounts):
    src_account, other_customer_account = cross_customer_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src_account)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(other_customer_account)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src_account, "target_account_id": other_customer_account, "amount": "100.00"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (403, 422), f"Expected 403 or 422, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") in ("unauthorized", "account_ownership_mismatch")

    assert api_client.get_balance(src_account) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(other_customer_account) == tgt_bal_before

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_declined
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account has balance >= $30,000
Target account belongs to the same customer
Customer has already transferred $20,000 today (daily total = $20,000)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=10000.00 (cumulative total would be $30,000)
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-053: $25,000 per day limit applies to cumulative daily total, not per-transaction
DT: condition combination — (valid amount per transaction) AND (cumulative > 25000) => decline
tests.executable:
def test_internal_transfer_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_declined(api_client, own_accounts, seed_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    seed_daily_transfers(src, tgt, amount=20000.00)  # <helper_fn>: seeds 20k already used today
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "10000.00"}
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_daily_transfer_total(src) == 20000.00

tests.name: test_internal_transfer_state_transition_pending_to_completed
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token available via <auth_fixture>
Source account has balance >= $500
Target account belongs to the same customer
No prior transfers today (daily total = $0)
actions: Poll until terminal state or timeout (max 5s)
expected: Source balance decremented atomically when status reaches 'completed'
No intermediate state leaves balances inconsistent
trace: STC: transfer lifecycle states — pending -> completed (happy path)
tests.executable:
import time

def test_internal_transfer_state_transition_pending_to_completed(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/internal",
        json={"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "500.00"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    transfer_id = resp.json()["transfer_id"]
    initial_status = resp.json()["status"]
    assert initial_status in ("pending", "completed")

    deadline = time.time() + 5
    status = initial_status
    while status != "completed" and time.time() < deadline:
        poll = api_client.get(f"/transfers/internal/{transfer_id}")
        status = poll.json()["status"]
        time.sleep(0.2)

    assert status == "completed", f"Transfer did not reach 'completed' within 5s, last status: {status}"
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before - 500.00
